questions.question: Congratulations! You have just passed your private pilot checkride! You are excited to take the FBO's trusty, if not slightly worn out, C182 for a few circuits around the airfield to exercise your PIC muscle. Your friend, Bonnie Propwash, comes along. After calculating the weight and balance, you realize you're going to need to add some ballast to the cargo area. The FBO has a bucket of ACA (American Cornhole Association) approved bags just for this purpose. They weigh 16 ounces each. What is the minimum number of cornhole bags you need to strap down in the cargo area to make the aircraft safe for flight? Here are the remaining details: [[TABLE]]

Your friend just took delivery of a Piper Navajo that was previously owned by the NFL's greatest quarterback, Snap McPigskin. Upon review of the manuals, you've discovered all the performance charts were converted to yards instead of feet, likely because yards were McPigskin's favorite unit on the gridiron. You calculate a takeoff run of 563 yards. Your runway is 4,300' long. How much distance remains once you become airborne?

On a VFR cross-country you notice that the actual winds aloft do not seem to match what you planned with. You take some notes and run some calculations obtaining the following information: TAS 154 knots; flying heading 233° you drift 6° left of course and have a ground speed of 145 knots; flying heading 308° you drift 2° right and have a groundspeed of 300 km/hr. What are the actual winds at your altitude?
You cross 16 radials in 12.6 minutes. Your airspeed on an arc is 208 km/hr. How far are you from the VOR station?
While planning for a practice VFR Navigation flight, you figure your time to fly 1 statute mile to be 19 seconds. What is your indicated airspeed on a calm wind, standard day at 3,500' MSL?

Your wealthy relative just purchased a new Citation for their family business and they want you to fly it! You just need a few more hours of PIC time to qualify for the insurance minimums. A warm IMC day is coming and you decide to take your safety pilot up to log some actual IMC and PIC. You've got the Grumman Cheetah fueled up and ready to go. You plan to land with 1 hour fuel reserve. The aircraft has a usable fuel capacity of 38 U.S. gallons and burns 6.8 GPH in cruise. The winds are 226° at 27 kts. You plan to fly a TC of 340° at 122 KTAS. What is your radius of action for today's out-and-back flight?

You are standing at the threshold of runway 16R as a Gulfstream G650 flies overhead. At an approach speed of 139 knots and a length of 99 feet 9 inches, how long does it take from the nose passing over to the tail passing over?

You are on a night cross-country heading to Smalltown Municipal Airport. The only airplane available tonight is the old one. The one without the GPS. The only avionics are an old VOR and an ILS receiver. It's ok though, because you know how to dead reckon and you have a new sectional downloaded on your EFB. En route to your first checkpoint you notice that you are a few miles right of course, 3.7 SM to be exact. You have flown 27 NM of 118 NM. You have been flying heading 128° so far. What heading do you fly to proceed directly to your destination?
Convert the following: 30.30" Hg = ___ mb.
In order to accurately request fuel while flying internationally, how many liters of fuel are equivalent to 25,500 pounds of fuel assuming one gallon of fuel weighs 6.75 pounds?
While making a turn around a point at 85 knots, the distance traveled in one full turn was 4.4 NM. How far from the point was maintained on average?
Referencing the previous question, how long will it take to do four full turns around that point?
questions.context: Referenced question 11: While making a turn around a point at 85 knots, the distance traveled in one full turn was 4.4 NM. How far from the point was maintained on average?

questions.question: Your aircraft (3,350 LBS) is packed full, but unfortunately the CG (49") is too far aft of the limit of 48". How much weight do you have to remove from the baggage compartment (station 122) to be 1" forward of the limit?

You touch down at 72 KIAS, and come to a complete stop in 9 seconds. If the field elevation is 4,216', altimeter setting is 29.67" Hg, and temperature is 62°F, what was your rate of deceleration after touchdown?
How many pounds of jet fuel have been burned between two checkpoints 188 NM apart if the aircraft is flying at 433 knots with a total burn rate of 4,160 pounds per hour?

As the wind howls at the window, you consider the crosswind limitations of your airplane. Alas, you are just a student pilot, and you have been given a crosswind limitation of 7 knots and tailwind limitation of 3 knots. The METAR reports the wind as 130° at 14 knots, and the variation is 7°E. The following runways are available: 17R, 22L, 35C, and 08. Which runway(s) will satisfy your solo requirement?

choices.text: None of the runways, try again tomorrow.

questions.correctChoice: None of the runways, try again tomorrow.

questions.question: The jet stream at FL310 (ISA-15°C) is 120° at 105 knots, whereas at FL270 (ISA-11°C), it is 160° at 143 knots. On a true course of 210° and an indicated airspeed of 450 knots, how much faster will it be to fly 1,817 KM at FL310 instead of FL270?

At 7 NM, an Airbus A321 is beginning the descent and will maintain a final approach ground speed of 148 knots. At 3 NM, a Piper Seneca is maintaining a ground speed of 110 knots. How far out on final will the Airbus be when the Seneca crosses the threshold?
What is the density altitude at 6,500 feet indicated if the pressure is 993 mb and the temperature is -34°F?

What should your airspeed indicator read if your aircraft crosses 6 NM in 2.7 minutes while flying a true heading of 312° holding a wind correction angle of 10° right? Wind is 020° at 24 knots. Temperature is ISA at 8,500' MSL.
Not considering wind, if you flew between 2 radials in 26 seconds at 97 knots, how long would it take to turn and fly 21 NM directly to the station?

You are flying a 172 NM flight on a true course of 104° at a true airspeed of 127 knots. If the winds aloft are 230° at 17 knots, how long will you have been flying when the time to continue equals the time to return?
Your current ramp weight is 2,437 LBS and your CG is 71.4". If the forward CG limit is 73", how many 5-KG ballast weights are required in the baggage compartment (94") to move the CG into the limit?
If you fly a 34 DME arc from the 282° radial clockwise to the 342° radial, how much excess distance did you fly compared to a straight line between the start and end points of the arc?

There is a 485' obstruction 2,451' from the departure end of Runway 14 (5,400' long). Your uncorrected ground roll is 823' and the performance chart states "Reduce ground roll by 4% for every 10 knots of headwind." Winds are 110° at 9 knots. What rate of climb is required to clear the obstacle by 50'?

Your flight will be 93 NM on a true course of 257°. You will burn 13.2 GPH while flying 151 KCAS at an indicated altitude of 5,500'. Winds aloft are 120° at 27 knots, the altimeter setting is 30.17" Hg, and the OAT is 4°C. How many pounds of Avgas are required to complete the flight with a 30-minute reserve?

You enter oceanic airspace over KEEKA at 1553 Zulu. ATC assigns you a cruise speed of Mach 0.78 at FL370 burning 6,000 pounds of fuel per hour. ATC asks what time you will be able to climb to FL380. Your computer shows that you weigh 161,000 pounds and that FL370 is your maximum altitude currently. After some research you note that at 150,000 pounds you'll be able to climb to FL380. What time do you tell ATC?
Convert 512 fluid ounces to liters.
Convert 38 meters per second to feet per second.
A temperature of 17°F is equivalent to ___°C.

The NIFA Metering judge needs a minimum of 9 minutes of separation between aircraft on a Navigation Event route. If a course is 72 NM long and an aircraft planning 120 KTAS is flying behind an aircraft planning 85 KTAS, how many seconds apart do the departure times need to be to have that separation the whole flight?
If an aircraft climbs at 670 FPM at a groundspeed of 96 knots, how high will it have climbed after 14 minutes?
At the end of a 288 NM cross-country flight, you refill the airplane with 19.4 gallons of fuel. If your average groundspeed was 103 knots, what was your average fuel burn rate?
What is the true airspeed equivalent of Mach 0.75 at FL410 with OAT of -56°C?
Assuming IAS = CAS, what would the airspeed indicator read for an aircraft at 9,300' pressure altitude, temperature of 10°F, 165 KTAS, and calm winds?
What is the groundspeed in climb?
questions.context: Use the following information to solve questions 36-40. An aircraft leaves their departure airport (Elev. 1,076') on a true course of 160° for 114 NM to their destination airport (Elev. 661'). Departure winds are 185° at 12 knots; destination winds are calm. Winds aloft at a cruise altitude of 7,500' MSL are 285° at 21 knots. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: How long will the aircraft be level in cruise?

questions.question: How many gallons of fuel are required if a 30-minute VFR reserve (cruise fuel burn) is included?
